method: node --input-type=module - <<'EOF' (SELECT ka.karyawan_id, ka.nip, k.pin AS k_pin FROM tb_karyawan_auth ka JOIN tb_karyawan k ON ka.karyawan_id = k.id WHERE ka.nip IN ('admin001','leader001','employee001') AND ka.is_active = 1 ORDER BY ka.nip; for each matching tb_user run the same canonical role mapping logic as lib/auth-session.ts, then compare sets)
details: Canonical roles are derived exactly as in lib/auth-session.ts: admin if is_admin or privilege>=4, group_leader when is_leader/is_hr/can_schedule true, employee otherwise, and 'employee' is appended when can_dashboard true. Legacy role labels (viewer, scheduler, etc.) are mapped via LEGACY_EMPLOYEE_ROLE_TO_CANONICAL_ROLE before deduping.
All three seeded identities (admin001, leader001, employee001) produced identical canonical role sets on both the NIP and PIN paths, so parity now passes.
